name: USS Croatan (ii) (CVE 25)
events: 12 Jun 1944 German U-boat U-490 was sunk north-west of the Azores in position 42°47'N, 40°08'W by depth charges from the US escort carrier USS Croatan, and depth charges from the destroyer escorts USS Frost, USS Huse and USS Inch.

1 Nov 1944 From 1 to 5 November 1944, Giovanni da Procida (T.V.(Lt.) F. Castracane Degli Antelminelli) conducted exercises off Bermuda with Task Group 22.5 which was made up of the escort carrier USS Croatan (T/Capt. J.P.W. Vest, USN, also COMTASKGR 22.5) and the destroyer escorts USS Frost (Lt.Cdr. A.E. Ritchie, USNR, with COMCORTDIV 13, T/Cdr. F.D. Giambattista, USN on board), USS Huse (T/Lt.Cdr. J.H. Batcheller, Jr., USN), USS Inch (Lt.Cdr. D.A. Tufts, USNR), USS Snowden (Lt.Cdr. N.W. Swanson, USNR), USS Stanton (Lt.Cdr. P.J. Tiffany, USNR) and USS Swasey (Lt.Cdr. H.A. White, USNR). The patrol vessel USS PCE-847 (Lt. G.B. Gross, USNR) was also in the area as close escort for the submarine.
Bad weather was encountered during 1-2 November causing delay to the exercise programme.

19 Jan 1945 From 19 to 23 Janaury 1945, Giovanni da Procida (T.V.(Lt.) F. Castracane Degli Antelminelli) conducted exercises off Bermuda with Task Group 22.5 which was made up of the escort carrier USS Croatan (T/Capt. J.P.W. Vest, USN, also COMTASKGR 22.5) and the destroyer escorts USS Frost (Lt.Cdr. A.E. Ritchie, USNR, with COMCORTDIV 13, T/Cdr. F.D. Giambattista, USN on board), USS Huse (T/Lt.Cdr. J.H. Batcheller, Jr., USN), USS Inch (Lt.Cdr. D.A. Tufts, USNR), USS Snowden (Lt.Cdr. N.W. Swanson, USNR), USS Stanton (Lt.Cdr. P.J. Tiffany, USNR) and USS Swasey (Lt.Cdr. H.A. White, USNR). The patrol vessel USS PCE-847 (Lt. T.K. Baldwin, USNR) was also in the area as close escort for the submarine.
In the evening of the 19th, USS Huse, USS Inch and USS Stanton were detached for aircraft rescue duties in the North Atlantic. They did not participate any further in the exercises.
